fields.description:
## Summary
Convergence rollout for epic 3006-e198: land the SAFE derisk code for the four drift-class heals so the reconciler can be rolled out level-triggered without changing arbitration. Deployment context: `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` runs `rebar reconcile --mode live` every 20 min from main; the workflow_dispatch `mode` input is the rollback brake (pin to dry-run).

## Scope (RE-SCOPED 2026-07-03)
This ticket's acceptance is the **in-repo code deliverable** — the derisk mechanism (baseline dual-write shadow), the circuit-breaker enforcement in the level-triggered apply path, and the rollback documentation — all landed on main and unit-tested. The **operational tail** (>= 10 clean live shadow passes, the Phase-3 prev_snapshot->baseline consumer-flag flip, and the capped live heal of the 7 known drift instances) requires live production Jira and the deployed 20-min workflow, so it CANNOT run in an agent session; it is carved out to follow-up **lofty-beech-twang (a118)** and owned by the deployed-workflow operator. Design for the full rollout (Phases 1-4, ADRs 0026-0029) is retained on a118.

## What landed on main (this ticket)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/baseline_shadow.py`: Phase-1 baseline dual-write SHADOW behind `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` — advances a per-binding baseline every pass and logs `baseline_shadow_check` (equal/divergent/seeded over 5 mirrored fields), emitting `baseline_shadow_divergence` on any mismatch, consumed by no one (arbitration unchanged). Wired in `reconcile.py` `_persist_and_log`.
- Circuit breaker `check_blast_radius` (config `reconciler.max_acting_fraction`, default 0.10) enforced in `binding_walk.py` BEFORE the first acting mutation; refuses the whole pass and emits a bridge alert when the acting fraction is exceeded.
- Rollback procedure documented in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` (workflow mode brake, breaker, dual-write shadow).
- Unit tests: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_baseline_shadow.py` (5 cells), breaker-refuse cells in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_binding_walk.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Baseline dual-write shadow behind `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` emits per-pass `baseline_shadow_check` logging and `baseline_shadow_divergence` on divergence, consumes no arbitration input (prev_snapshot untouched); covered by `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_baseline_shadow.py`. — landed on main.
- [ ] Circuit breaker enforced in the level-triggered apply path (`binding_walk.py`): a pass whose acting fraction exceeds `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (default 0.10) refuses BEFORE the first mutation and emits an alert; covered by the breaker-refuse cells in `test_binding_walk.py`. — landed on main.
- [ ] Rollback procedure (pin workflow `mode` input to dry-run) documented in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`. — landed on main.
- [ ] The operational tail (>= 10 clean live shadow passes, Phase-3 consumer-flag flip, capped live heal of the 7 known instances, next-pass census = 0) is carved to follow-up lofty-beech-twang (a118) with full design preserved. — done.
